description_long: This stone block features a geometric, angular form with a prominent beveled top edge and a distinct notch on one side, giving it a modern, utilitarian appearance. The surfaces are flat and sharply defined, with a consistent medium-gray coloration that suggests it is made from concrete or a similar dense stone material. The block's top surface is slightly sloped and faceted, while the sides are vertical and smooth, with no visible seams or joints. Its overall shape is irregular, with one end extending outward, possibly to interlock with adjacent blocks or fit a specific urban design. The finish is matte, with a subtle texture that hints at cast or molded fabrication. There are no visible markings, engravings, or signs of significant weathering, indicating it is relatively new or well-maintained.
description: A modern, angular gray stone block with a beveled top and a notched side, featuring smooth, matte surfaces and a solid, utilitarian appearance.
description_view_0: The block's notched side and beveled top edge are clearly visible, highlighting its irregular, angular shape.
description_view_1: This view emphasizes the block's vertical face and the sloped top, with the notch less prominent.
description_view_2: The longest, uninterrupted side of the block is visible, showing the full extent of the beveled top surface.
description_view_3: The notched section is most apparent, with the block's angular geometry and smooth surfaces in clear view.
attribute_installation_method: set in concrete or on compacted gravel base
attribute_weathering_pattern: no visible weathering; surfaces appear clean and well-maintained
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or aligned with other blocks as a barrier
